attacking_style: Launches long-range solo attacks (30km+) on decisive climbs, relying on sustained power output rather than explosive accelerations. Capable of maintaining winning gaps through pure time-trialing ability.
team_tactics: Benefits from tactical hesitation and disorganization among chasers rather than explicit team dominance. Attacks appear individually driven with limited visible domestique support in final.
terrain_preference: Ardennes-type terrain combining punchy climbs with long technical descents/flats where TT power maintains gaps. Excels when races demand sustained watts over explosive climbing.
vulnerability: Success depends on field fragmentation and chasing hesitation; organized pursuit by strong TT riders could neutralize long-range efforts before finish.
key_patterns: Elite time-trial speed (104.85kph peak) serves as foundation for both stage race and one-day success.
Willing to commit to audacious long-distance solos, sustaining 40-48 second gaps over 30km+.
Precocious monument winner at 22, demonstrating grand tour power reserves unusual for age category.
form_trajectory: positive: Monument victory in April followed by dominant TT performance in June shows consistent progression through 2022 season
